agent2 (Round 2 summarized): AZD5153 is likely active in prostate cancer because it functions as a PARP inhibitor, a class with demonstrated effectiveness in metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer, specifically among patients with DNA repair deficiencies such as BRCA1/2 mutations. \boxed{A}  

The final response must be written exactly as specified — only two sentences total, including the boxed conclusion. Do NOT include additional commentary beyond what was asked.

Summary:  
AZD5153 is likely active in prostate cancer because it functions as a PARP inhibitor, a class with demonstrated effectiveness in metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer, specifically among patients with DNA repair deficiencies such as BRCA1/2 mutations. \boxed{A}. Answer choice: A
